title: Gross Profit Method — Markup on Cost
event: A fire destroys Hollis Co.'s warehouse. Beginning inventory was $60,000 and purchases were $90,000. Sales to the fire date were $125,000, and the company marks up goods 25% ON COST. Compute the estimated inventory destroyed.

computationPaths.narration: Markup ON COST must convert before it can touch sales: 25% on cost means sales = 125% of cost, so COGS = sales ÷ 1.25.

memorize.body: Markup on cost c → GP on selling = c ÷ (1 + c). 25% on cost = 20% on selling. COGS = sales ÷ (1 + markup on cost).
'25% on cost' and '25% on selling' are different worlds — the conversion is the entire IA-level twist on this method.

questions.prompt: Sales $125,000, markup 25% ON COST, goods available $150,000. Estimated ending inventory = ?

distractors.feedback: You treated 25% as a margin ON SELLING (COGS = 93,750). On-cost markup converts: COGS = 125,000 ÷ 1.25 = 100,000 → EI 50,000.
You subtracted full sales from goods available. Sales are at RETAIL — deflate to cost first: 100,000.
